# path-to-regexp

[![NPM version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![Build status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]
[![Dependency Status][david-image]][david-url]
[![License][license-image]][license-url]

> Turn an Express-style path string such as `/user/:name` into a regular expression.

## Installation

```
npm install path-to-regexp --save
```

## Usage

```javascript
var pathToRegexp = require('path-to-regexp');

// Using strings:
var keys = [];
var re = pathToRegexp('/foo/:bar', keys);
// re = /^\/foo\/([^\/]+?)\/?$/i
// keys = [{ name: 'bar', prefix: '/', delimiter: '/', optional: false, repeat: false, pattern: '[^\/]+?' }]

// Using array of strings:
var re = pathToRegexp(['/foo/:bar', '/foo/:baz']);
// re = /^\/foo\/([^\/]+?)\/?$/i

// Using RegExp:
var re = pathToRegexp(/^\/foo\//);
// re = /^\/foo\//i

// With options:
var re = pathToRegexp('/foo/:bar', keys, { sensitive: true });
// re = /^\/foo\/([^\/]+?)\/?$/
// keys = [{ name: 'bar', prefix: '/', delimiter: '/', optional: false, repeat: false, pattern: '[^\/]+?' }]

// Match URLs and extract params:
var match = pathToRegexp.match('/user/:id');
var params = match('/user/123');
// params = { id: '123' }
```

### Parameters

The path string can contain parameters denoted by a colon `:` or an asterisk `*`. Named parameters are defined by prefixing a colon to the parameter name (`:foo`). Unnamed parameters are defined by using an asterisk (`*`).

#### Named Parameters

Named parameters are defined by prefixing a colon to the parameter name (`:foo`).

```javascript
var re = pathToRegexp('/:foo/:bar', keys);
// keys = [{ name: 'foo', ... }, { name: 'bar', ... }]

re.exec('/test/route');
//=> ['/test/route', 'test', 'route']
```

#### Parameter Modifiers

Parameters can be suffixed with modifiers:

* `?` - Makes the parameter optional (zero or one)
* `+` - Makes the parameter required (one or more)
* `*` - Makes the parameter optional (zero or more)

```javascript
// Optional parameter
var re = pathToRegexp('/:foo?', keys);
// keys = [{ name: 'foo', delimiter: '/', optional: true, repeat: false }]

re.exec('/');
//=> ['/', undefined]

re.exec('/bar');
//=> ['/bar', 'bar']

// Required parameter
var re = pathToRegexp('/:foo+', keys);
// keys = [{ name: 'foo', delimiter: '/', optional: false, repeat: true }]

re.exec('/');
//=> null

re.exec('/bar');
//=> ['/bar', 'bar']

re.exec('/bar/baz');
//=> ['/bar/baz', 'bar/baz']

// Zero or more parameters
var re = pathToRegexp('/:foo*', keys);
// keys = [{ name: 'foo', delimiter: '/', optional: true, repeat: true }]

re.exec('/');
//=> ['/', undefined]

re.exec('/bar');
//=> ['/bar', 'bar']

re.exec('/bar/baz');
//=> ['/bar/baz', 'bar/baz']
```

#### Custom Parameter Patterns

Parameters can be provided a custom pattern using parentheses `(...)`. This allows for more advanced parameter matching, such as restricting to digits or specific string patterns.

```javascript
var re = pathToRegexp('/:foo(\\d+)', keys);
// keys = [{ name: 'foo', ... }]

re.exec('/123');
//=> ['/123', '123']

re.exec('/abc');
//=> null
```

### Options

* **sensitive** - When `true` the regexp will be case sensitive. (default: `false`)
* **strict** - When `true` the regexp won't allow an optional trailing delimiter to match. (default: `false`)
* **end** - When `true` the regexp will match to the end of the string. (default: `true`)
* **delimiter** - The default delimiter for segments. (default: `/`)

## Express Usage

This library is used by Express to match routes and extract parameters. Here's a simple example of how Express uses path-to-regexp internally:

```javascript
var express = require('express');
var app = express();

// Define a route with parameters
app.get('/user/:id', function(req, res) {
  // Access the parameter value
  var userId = req.params.id;
  res.send('User ID: ' + userId);
});

// Define a route with optional parameters
app.get('/posts/:year?/:month?/:day?', function(req, res) {
  var year = req.params.year || 'all years';
  var month = req.params.month || 'all months';
  var day = req.params.day || 'all days';
  
  res.send('Posts from ' + year + '/' + month + '/' + day);
});

// Define a route with a wildcard parameter
app.get('/files/*', function(req, res) {
  var path = req.params[0]; // Captures the entire path after /files/
  res.send('You requested files at: ' + path);
});

app.listen(3000);
```

## API

### pathToRegexp(path, [keys], [options])

Convert a path string, array of strings, or a regular expression into a regular expression that can be used for matching and parameter extraction.

* `path` - A string, array of strings, or a regular expression
* `keys` - An array to populate with parameter keys
* `options` - Options object (see above)

Returns a regular expression.

```javascript
var keys = [];
var re = pathToRegexp('/user/:id', keys);
// re = /^\/user\/([^\/]+?)\/?$/i
// keys = [{ name: 'id', prefix: '/', delimiter: '/', optional: false, repeat: false, pattern: '[^\/]+?' }]
```

### pathToRegexp.parse(path, [options])

Parse a path string into an array of tokens.

* `path` - A string to parse
* `options` - Options object (see above)

Returns an array of tokens.

```javascript
var tokens = pathToRegexp.parse('/route/:foo/(.*)');
// tokens = [
//   '/route',
//   { name: 'foo', prefix: '/', delimiter: '/', optional: false, repeat: false, pattern: '[^\/]+?' },
//   '/',
//   { name: 0, prefix: '', delimiter: '', optional: false, repeat: false, pattern: '.*' }
// ]
```

### pathToRegexp.compile(path, [options])

Compile a path string to a function for generating URLs with parameter values.

* `path` - A string to compile
* `options` - Options object (see above)

Returns a function for generating paths.

```javascript
var toPath = pathToRegexp.compile('/user/:id');

toPath({ id: 123 }); //=> "/user/123"
toPath({ id: 'café' }); //=> "/user/caf%C3%A9"
toPath({ id: '/' }); //=> "/user/%2F"

// Use `encode` option to customize encoding
toPath({ id: '/' }, { encode: function(value) { return value; } }); //=> "/user/"
```

### pathToRegexp.tokensToFunction(tokens, [options])

Transform an array of tokens into a path generator function.

* `tokens` - An array of tokens (from `pathToRegexp.parse`)
* `options` - Options object (see above)

Returns a function for generating paths.

```javascript
var tokens = pathToRegexp.parse('/user/:id');
var toPath = pathToRegexp.tokensToFunction(tokens);

toPath({ id: 123 }); //=> "/user/123"
```

### pathToRegexp.tokensToRegExp(tokens, [keys], [options])

Transform an array of tokens into a regular expression.

* `tokens` - An array of tokens (from `pathToRegexp.parse`)
* `keys` - An array to populate with parameter keys
* `options` - Options object (see above)

Returns a regular expression.

```javascript
var tokens = pathToRegexp.parse('/user/:id');
var keys = [];
var re = pathToRegexp.tokensToRegExp(tokens, keys);
// re = /^\/user\/([^\/]+?)\/?$/i
// keys = [{ name: 'id', prefix: '/', delimiter: '/', optional: false, repeat: false, pattern: '[^\/]+?' }]
```

### pathToRegexp.match(path)

Create a function for matching paths and extracting parameters.

* `path` - A path string or regular expression

Returns a function that takes a path and returns an object with parameter values or null if no match.

```javascript
var match = pathToRegexp.match('/user/:id');

match('/user/123'); //=> { id: '123' }
match('/user/abc'); //=> { id: 'abc' }
match('/post/123'); //=> null
```

## Express.js Integration

This library is a core component of Express.js routing. When you define routes in Express, path-to-regexp is used internally to:

1. Convert route patterns (like `/user/:id`) into regular expressions
2. Extract parameter names from route patterns
3. Match incoming request URLs against these regular expressions
4. Extract parameter values from matched URLs

For example, in the "hello_world" microservice that uses Express.js, the following routes might be defined:

```javascript
app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Hello, World!\n');
});

app.get('/evening', (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Good evening\n');
});
```

Internally, Express uses path-to-regexp to convert these route patterns into regular expressions that can match incoming request URLs.
